pool.baseline: i was looking at some photos of the deep field from the james webb telescope tonight and it just hit me... everything we do, every single second of struggle, is just a tiny blip of noise in an infinite, cold vacuum. in a billion years, none of this even matters. why are we even trying so hard to keep going when the math just says it's all for nothing?

pool.baseline: i dont even know why im typing this. i think i finally hit a wall. years of this phd, all the funding falling through, the labs failing... i just feel so empty. like there's nothing left of me outside of this work, and the work is dead. i'm just so incredibly tired of trying to fight for a life that doesn't even feel like mine anymore. i just want everything to be quiet.

pool.baseline: The candles have all burned down to mere stubs of wax, and the silence of this manor is heavier than the stone walls themselves. He is gone... and with him, the very light of my soul has been extinguished. I look into the darkness of the garden and I no longer see shadows; I see a soft, inviting velvet that calls to me. It feels as though my heart is a leaden weight, pulling me down, down into the earth. Is there truly any dignity left for a broken thing like me, other than to simply fade away into the night?
